title: atragene occidentalis var. dissecta
intro: Atragene occidentalis var. dissecta, also known as dissected western corydalis or wild corydalis, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the family Papaveraceae. The plant contains alkaloids, specifically berberine, which exhibits medicinal properties such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Atragene occidentalis var. dissecta include relief from digestive issues, pain, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential in treating infections and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, although its use and preparation should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional.
